questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The highest common factor (HCF) of $24$ and $36$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** HCF is the product of common prime factors at lowest powers.

**Working.** $24=2^3\cdot 3$, $36=2^2\cdot 3^2$; HCF $=2^2\cdot 3=12$.

**Answer.** $12$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The area of a rectangle of length $12$ and width $5$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Area of rectangle $=\text{length}\times\text{width}$.

**Working.** $12\times 5 = 60$.

**Answer.** $60$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: An amount of $600$ is divided in the ratio $2:3$. The larger part is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each share equals the total times its ratio fraction.

**Working.** Larger fraction $=\dfrac{3}{2+3}=\dfrac{3}{5}$, so
$$600\times\frac{3}{5}=360$$

**Answer.** $360$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Select the antonym of 'TRANSPARENT'.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Transparent' means allowing light to pass through so objects are visible.

**Working.** The opposite is 'opaque' (not allowing light through); the other options are near-synonyms of transparent.

**Answer.** opaque.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The square root of $196$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Find the number whose square is $196$.

**Working.** $14^2 = 196$.

**Answer.** $14$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem:
Arrange the sentences into a coherent paragraph.
P: As a result, the roof was reinforced with additional bolts.
Q: During inspection, engineers noticed hairline cracks in the roof.
R: They immediately evacuated the section as a precaution.
S: A detailed survey then confirmed that the rock was unstable.
The correct order is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Arrange by cause-and-effect and chronological cues.

**Working.** Cracks are noticed (Q), the area is evacuated as a precaution (R), a survey confirms instability (S), and as a result bolts are added (P).

**Answer.** Q, R, S, P.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: If $12$ workers build a wall in $10$ days, how many days will $15$ workers (of equal efficiency) take to build the same wall?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Workers and time are inversely proportional for a fixed task.

**Working.**
$$\text{days}=\frac{12\times 10}{15}=8$$

**Answer.** $8$ days.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The three angles of a triangle are in the ratio $1:2:3$. The largest angle is _____ degrees.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Angles of a triangle sum to $180^\circ$.

**Working.** Parts $=1+2+3=6$; largest $=\dfrac{3}{6}\times 180 = 90^\circ$.

**Answer.** $90$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A water image is a reflection about a horizontal line. Which digit looks the same as its water image?

figure.caption: The digit 8 with a dashed horizontal reflection line through its middle.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 140 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><text x='50' y='88' font-size='80' fill='#334155'>8</text><line x1='15' y1='60' x2='125' y2='60' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='4 3'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** A digit unchanged by a horizontal flip has a horizontal axis of symmetry.

**Working.** '8' is symmetric top-to-bottom, so its water image is identical; 2, 5 and 7 are not.

**Answer.** 8.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A boat covers $16$ km downstream in $2$ hours and returns the same distance upstream in $4$ hours. The speed of the boat in still water is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Boat speed = (downstream + upstream)/2.

**Working.** Downstream $=\tfrac{16}{2}=8$, upstream $=\tfrac{16}{4}=4$ km/h; boat $=\dfrac{8+4}{2}=6$ km/h.

**Answer.** $6$ km/h.

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A hyperaccumulator crop yields $20\,\text{t/ha}$ of dry biomass containing $500\,\text{mg/kg}$ of nickel. The nickel phytoextracted per hectare in one harvest is _____ g/ha.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Metal extracted $=$ dry biomass $\times$ tissue concentration. With biomass $20\,\text{t/ha}=20{,}000\,\text{kg/ha}$ and $500\,\text{mg/kg}$: $(20{,}000)(500)/1000$ g.

**Working.**
$$20{,}000\times500/1000=10{,}000\,\text{g/ha}.$$

**Answer.** $10\,000\,\text{g/ha}$

**Concept.** CO₂-e = mass × GWP, with the GWP read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$3\times28=84.$$

**Answer.** $84$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A field device estimates wastewater BOD within minutes by measuring the respiration of immobilised microbes through a dissolved-oxygen electrode. The biological recognition element and the transducer are, respectively,
options: immobilised microorganisms and the dissolved-oxygen electrode
the DO electrode and the microbes
questions.solution:
**Concept.** In a microbial BOD biosensor the immobilised microbes are the bio-recognition element; the O₂ they consume is sensed by the DO electrode (the transducer).

**Working.**
Recognition = microbes; transducer = DO electrode.

**Answer.** Microbes; DO electrode

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Wet MSW $1500\,\text{kg}$ at $60\%$ moisture. The dry-solids mass is _____ kg.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dry = wet × (1 − moisture).

**Working.**
$$1500(1-60/100)=600.$$

**Answer.** $600\,\text{kg}$

**Concept.** Each transfer retains ~10%: $E_n=E_0(0.1)^n$, with $E_0$ read from the pyramid.

**Working.**
$$8000\times0.1^{2}=80.$$

**Answer.** $80\,\text{kcal/m}^2/\text{yr}$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: $2$ identical sources each $85\,\text{dB}$. Combined level is _____ dB.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** L = L₁ + 10log n.

**Working.**
$$85+10\log2=88.01.$$

**Answer.** $88.01\,\text{dB}$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: A municipality proposes to mine its sole confined aquifer to exhaustion within 15 years to bankroll current expansion, leaving the next generation with no water source. This plan MOST directly violates which pillar of sustainable development?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Exhausting a resource so future generations cannot meet their own needs breaches inter-generational equity — the core of the Brundtland definition.

**Working.**
Map the scenario to the violated principle.

**Answer.** Inter-generational equity

figure.caption: Sequential stages of the EIA process.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** The EMP details mitigation, monitoring and institutional arrangements.

**Working.**
EMP follows impact prediction.

**Answer.** EMP

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: In a reactor, $40\,\text{mg/L}$ of biomass is produced while $100\,\text{mg/L}$ of substrate is consumed. The observed yield $Y_{X/S}$ is _____ mg/mg.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $Y_{X/S}=\dfrac{\Delta X}{\Delta S}$.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{40}{100}=0.4.$$

**Answer.** $0.4$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: $3$ identical sources each $70\,\text{dB}$. Combined level is _____ dB.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** L = L₁ + 10log n.

**Working.**
$$70+10\log3=74.77.$$

**Answer.** $74.77\,\text{dB}$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: In the settling-column test plotted in the figure, a particle settles the marked depth in the marked time. Its settling velocity is _____ mm/s.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 200" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="46" y2="170" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="300" y2="20" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="6" y="100" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">depth ↓</text><text x="252" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="270" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><line x1="270" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="46" y1="150" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="150" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">t = 400 s</text><text x="118" y="165" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">settles 2.5 m</text></svg>
figure.caption: Particle settles the marked depth in the marked time; v_s = depth ÷ time. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** v_s = depth ÷ time (read both from the trajectory).

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{2.5\,\text{m}}{400\,\text{s}}\times1000=6.25\,\text{mm/s}.$$

**Answer.** $6.25\,\text{mm/s}$

questions.stem: A facility emits CO₂, CH₄ (GWP 28) and N₂O (GWP 265); the three masses (t) are shown on the bar chart. The total carbon footprint is _____ t CO₂-e.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 188" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="36" y1="150" x2="304" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="60" y="30" width="46" height="120" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="83" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">80</text><text x="83" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CO₂</text><rect x="142" y="144" width="46" height="6" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="165" y="140" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">4</text><text x="165" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CH₄</text><rect x="224" y="144" width="46" height="6" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="247" y="140" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">2</text><text x="247" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">N₂O</text><text x="170" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">t</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Σ massᵢ × GWPᵢ, with the three masses read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$80+4\times28+2\times265=722.$$

**Answer.** $722$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: At a discount rate of $8\%$, and reading the future benefit $F$ and its time horizon $n$ from the cash-flow diagram, the present worth is $₹$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 150" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="30" y1="100" x2="310" y2="100" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><text x="30" y="118" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">0</text><line x1="290" y1="100" x2="290" y2="48" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.6"/><path d="M290 46 l-5 9 10 0 z" fill="#16a34a"/><text x="290" y="40" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">F = ₹50,000</text><text x="290" y="118" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">year 4</text><text x="165" y="118" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">time (years) →</text><text x="42" y="60" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">PW = ?</text></svg>
figure.caption: Future benefit F and its time horizon n marked on the cash-flow diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** PW = F/(1+i)^n, with F and n read from the diagram.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{50000}{(1+0.08)^{4}}=36751.$$

**Answer.** $₹36751$

questions.stem: A population grows exponentially at $r=0.05\,\text{yr}^{-1}$. Reading the initial size $N_0$ from the figure, after $11$ yr, $N=$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 190" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="20" x2="40" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="40" y1="165" x2="300" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><path d="M40 150 C120 145 180 120 230 80 C260 55 285 35 300 25" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="40" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="46" y="146" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">N₀ = 500</text><text x="14" y="30" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">N</text><text x="278" y="180" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** N = N₀e^{rt}, with N₀ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$500e^{0.05\times11}=867.$$

**Answer.** $867$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: A discrete particle ($\rho_s=2650\,\text{kg/m}^3$) settles in water ($\rho=1000$, $\mu=10^{-3}\,\text{Pa·s}$). Reading its diameter from the figure, its terminal velocity is _____ ×10⁻³ m/s.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 180" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><rect x="60" y="20" width="180" height="140" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.06)" stroke="#1e293b"/><circle cx="150" cy="66" r="12" fill="#94a3b8" stroke="#1e293b"/><line x1="138" y1="66" x2="162" y2="66" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1"/><text x="168" y="70" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">d = 0.02 mm</text><path d="M150 82 l0 52 m-4 -6 l4 6 4 -6" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><text x="158" y="118" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">v_s</text><text x="150" y="174" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">ρ_s = 2650 kg/m³, water μ = 10⁻³ Pa·s</text></svg>
figure.caption: Particle diameter marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $v_s=\dfrac{g(\rho_s-\rho)d^2}{18\mu}$, with $d$ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{9.81\times1650\times(0.00002)^2}{18\times10^{-3}}=0.00036\,\text{m/s}.$$

**Answer.** $0.36\times10^{-3}$

questions.stem: An ESP treats $4\,\text{m}^3/\text{s}$. Reading the collecting area $A$ and drift velocity $w$ from the figure, its collection efficiency is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 170" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="30" width="260" height="110" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.2"/><line x1="80" y1="30" x2="80" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="150" y1="30" x2="150" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="220" y1="30" x2="220" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="115" y1="35" x2="115" y2="135" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><line x1="185" y1="35" x2="185" y2="135" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><path d="M14 70 l22 0 m-6 -3 l6 3 -6 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="6" y="64" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">gas in</text><text x="60" y="158" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">collecting area A = 200 m²</text><text x="150" y="22" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">w = 0.12 m/s (drift)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Collecting area A and drift velocity w marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** η = 1 − e^{−wA/Q}, with A and w read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$(1-e^{-0.12\times200/4})\times100=99.75\%.$$

**Answer.** $99.75\%$

questions.stem: A refrigerant recovery releases $2\,\text{t}$ of CFC-11 (ODP $=1.0$) and $5\,\text{t}$ of HCFC-22 (ODP $=0.055$). The total release expressed as CFC-11-equivalent (ODP-weighted) is _____ t.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** ODP-weighted release $=\sum(\text{mass}\times\text{ODP})$ — the basis on which the Montreal Protocol controls ozone-depleting substances.

**Working.**
$$2(1.0)+5(0.055)=2.275\,\text{t}.$$

**Answer.** $2.275\,\text{t CFC-11-eq}$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: Which are recognised biodiversity hotspots in/around India?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Western Ghats, Himalaya and Indo-Burma are global hotspots; the Thar is not (low endemism/richness threshold).

**Working.**
First three.

**Answer.** Western Ghats, Himalaya, Indo-Burma

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: Anaerobic digestion of $1000\,\text{kg}$ of volatile solids yields biogas at $0.4\,\text{m}^3/\text{kg VS}$, of which $60\%$ is methane (calorific value $36\,\text{MJ/m}^3$). The recoverable energy is _____ MJ.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Energy $=$ VS $\times$ gas yield $\times$ CH₄ fraction $\times$ calorific value.

**Working.**
$$1000\times0.4\times0.6\times36=8640\,\text{MJ}.$$

**Answer.** $8640\,\text{MJ}$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A population grows logistically at $r=0.2\,\text{yr}^{-1}$. Reading the carrying capacity $K$ from the growth curve shown, the maximum $dN/dt$ ($rK/4$) is _____ /yr.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 190" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="34" y1="20" x2="34" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="34" y1="165" x2="305" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="34" y1="40" x2="305" y2="40" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="240" y="36" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">K = 8000</text><path d="M34 158 C120 156 150 80 175 60 C210 44 260 41 305 40" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><text x="10" y="30" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">N</text><text x="285" y="180" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text><text x="120" y="120" font-size="8" fill="#16a34a">max dN/dt at N=K/2</text></svg>
figure.caption: Carrying capacity K marked on the growth curve only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** max dN/dt = rK/4 at N=K/2, with K read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.2\times8000}{4}=400.$$

**Answer.** $400$

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: A culture follows Monod kinetics at substrate $S=40\,\text{mg/L}$. Reading $\mu_{max}$ and $K_s$ from the $\mu$–$S$ curve shown, the specific growth rate $\mu$ is _____ h⁻¹.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="44" y1="20" x2="44" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="44" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="240" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">substrate [S] →</text><line x1="44" y1="40" x2="312" y2="40" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="232" y="36" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">μ_max = 0.6</text><path d="M44 165 C90 110 140 70 200 56 C250 46 290 42 312 41" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="44" y1="102" x2="120" y2="102" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="120" y1="102" x2="120" y2="165" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="14" y="106" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">μ_max/2</text><text x="98" y="178" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">K_s = 50</text></svg>
figure.caption: Monod μ–S curve: μ_max (plateau) and K_s (half-saturation) marked on the curve only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** μ = μ_max S/(K_s+S), with μ_max and K_s read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.6\times40}{50+40}=0.267.$$

**Answer.** $0.267$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A population grows exponentially at $r=0.02\,\text{yr}^{-1}$. Reading the initial size $N_0$ from the figure, after $14$ yr, $N=$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 190" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="20" x2="40" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="40" y1="165" x2="300" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><path d="M40 150 C120 145 180 120 230 80 C260 55 285 35 300 25" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="40" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="46" y="146" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">N₀ = 500</text><text x="14" y="30" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">N</text><text x="278" y="180" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text></svg>
figure.caption: Initial population N₀ marked on the curve at t = 0. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** N = N₀e^{rt}, with N₀ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$500e^{0.02\times14}=662.$$

**Answer.** $662$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: $25\,\text{mL}$ of an acid is titrated with $0.05\,\text{N}$ NaOH. Reading the equivalence-point volume $V_{eq}$ from the titration curve shown, the normality of the acid is _____ N.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 195" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="18" x2="40" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="40" y1="165" x2="305" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="16" y="28" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">pH</text><text x="220" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">titrant volume (mL) →</text><path d="M40 150 C120 146 160 140 168 92 C176 44 230 36 305 30" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="168" y1="30" x2="168" y2="165" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="168" cy="92" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="118" y="70" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">equivalence</text><text x="158" y="178" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">25</text></svg>
figure.caption: Equivalence-point titrant volume marked on the x-axis only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** At the equivalence point $N_aV_a=N_bV_b$, with $V_{eq}$ read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$N_a=\dfrac{0.05\times25}{25}=0.05.$$

**Answer.** $0.05\,\text{N}$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: Which stage of the EIA cycle immediately follows screening?

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 360 90" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="8" y="28" width="60" height="34" rx="4" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="38" y="49" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Screening</text><path d="M68 45 l10 0 m-4 -3 l4 3 -4 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><rect x="78" y="28" width="60" height="34" rx="4" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="108" y="49" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Scoping</text><path d="M138 45 l10 0 m-4 -3 l4 3 -4 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><rect x="148" y="28" width="60" height="34" rx="4" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="178" y="44" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Impact</text><text x="178" y="55" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">prediction</text><path d="M208 45 l10 0 m-4 -3 l4 3 -4 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><rect x="218" y="28" width="60" height="34" rx="4" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="248" y="49" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">EMP</text><path d="M278 45 l10 0 m-4 -3 l4 3 -4 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><rect x="288" y="28" width="60" height="34" rx="4" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="318" y="44" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Review &</text><text x="318" y="55" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">clearance</text></svg>
figure.caption: Sequential stages of the EIA process.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Order: screening → scoping → impact prediction → EMP → review/clearance.

**Working.**
Scoping follows screening.

**Answer.** Scoping

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: At a discount rate of $8\%$, and reading the future benefit $F$ and its time horizon $n$ from the cash-flow diagram, the present worth is $₹$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 150" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="30" y1="100" x2="310" y2="100" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><text x="30" y="118" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">0</text><line x1="290" y1="100" x2="290" y2="48" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.6"/><path d="M290 46 l-5 9 10 0 z" fill="#16a34a"/><text x="290" y="40" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">F = ₹50,000</text><text x="290" y="118" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">year 6</text><text x="165" y="118" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">time (years) →</text><text x="42" y="60" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">PW = ?</text></svg>
figure.caption: Future benefit F and its time horizon n marked on the cash-flow diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** PW = F/(1+i)^n, with F and n read from the diagram.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{50000}{(1+0.08)^{6}}=31508.$$

**Answer.** $₹31508$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: Which gases are ozone-depleting substances?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** CFCs, halons and CCl₄ deplete ozone; CO₂ is a GHG but not an ODS.

**Working.**
First three.

**Answer.** CFCs, halons, CCl₄

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A source emits $5\,\text{t}$ of methane (GWP$_{100}=25$). Its carbon-dioxide-equivalent emission is _____ t CO₂-e.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 180" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="70" y="30" width="46" height="120" fill="#dc2626" opacity="0.75"/><text x="93" y="26" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">in 125</text><rect x="190" y="145.2" width="46" height="4.8" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.8"/><text x="213" y="141.2" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">out 5</text><text x="160" y="170" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CH₄ → CO₂-e (t)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Methane converted to CO₂-equivalent via GWP.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** CO₂-e $=$ mass $\times$ GWP.

**Working.**
$$5\times25=125.$$

**Answer.** $125\,\text{t CO}_2\text{-e}$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Decomposable waste of $2000\,\text{kg}$ yields landfill gas at $0.1\,\text{m}^3/\text{kg}$. The total gas generated is _____ m³.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 180" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="30" y="30" width="280" height="120" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.2"/><rect x="30" y="40" width="280" height="86" fill="#a16207" opacity="0.5"/><rect x="30" y="30" width="280" height="10" fill="#65a30d"/><rect x="30" y="126" width="280" height="14" fill="#1e3a8a" opacity="0.6"/><rect x="30" y="140" width="280" height="10" fill="#0f172a"/><text x="170" y="26" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">final cover / vegetation</text><text x="170" y="86" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">compacted MSW + daily cover</text><text x="170" y="137" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">leachate collection</text><text x="170" y="148" font-size="7" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">composite liner (clay+HDPE)</text><path d="M315 60 q14 0 14 14 q0 10 -8 18" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="318" y="56" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">gas vent</text></svg>
figure.caption: Engineered sanitary landfill: cover, waste cell, liner and leachate system.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Gas $=$ mass $\times$ specific yield.

**Working.**
$$2000\times0.1=200.$$

**Answer.** $200\,\text{m}^3$
